title: 2.7 Photosynthesis
description: Photosynthesis — every fact from the textbook.

questions.question: What are the leaves of green plants described as?
options: The largest food factories on Earth
questions.correctAnswer: The largest food factories on Earth

questions.question: What provides food that supports nearly all life on earth?
options: The product of leaves together with photosynthetic productivity of algae
questions.correctAnswer: The product of leaves together with photosynthetic productivity of algae

questions.question: Why are plants called producers or autotrophs?
options: They can prepare their own food by photosynthesis
questions.correctAnswer: They can prepare their own food by photosynthesis

questions.question: What term describes organisms that feed on plants and on one another?
What is the basic source of energy that sustains life?
What pigment absorbs sunlight in plants?
What does chlorophyll enable plants to do?
options: Trap solar energy and transform it into chemical energy
questions.correctAnswer: Trap solar energy and transform it into chemical energy

questions.question: What organisms bear chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis?
What is the photosynthetic apparatus?
What are the two distinct parts of a chloroplast?
What is a granum composed of?
options: Stacks of flattened sacks, each called thylakoid
questions.correctAnswer: Stacks of flattened sacks, each called thylakoid

questions.question: What does the granum contain?
options: Chlorophyll, enzymes and co-factors that participate in the light trapping phase of photosynthesis
questions.correctAnswer: Chlorophyll, enzymes and co-factors that participate in the light trapping phase of photosynthesis

questions.question: Where does the light reaction of photosynthesis take place?
What is the stroma of the chloroplast?
options: A gel-like colourless matrix that is the site for sugar synthesis
questions.correctAnswer: A gel-like colourless matrix that is the site for sugar synthesis

questions.question: What is produced in the stroma?
What organic compounds are directly or indirectly converted from sugar produced in the stroma?
What light absorbing pigments are found in the chloroplast?
options: Chlorophyll a and b and other accessory pigments
questions.correctAnswer: Chlorophyll a and b and other accessory pigments

questions.question: What range of visible light wavelengths do chloroplast pigments absorb?
At which wavelengths does maximum light absorption occur?
What colours correspond to the wavelengths of maximum absorption?
Why do leaves look green?
options: Light ranging from 500 to 600 nm (green light) is reflected, not absorbed
questions.correctAnswer: Light ranging from 500 to 600 nm (green light) is reflected, not absorbed

questions.question: What happens during the light reaction of photosynthesis?
options: Water molecules are split into $H^+$ and $O_2$ (photolysis) and electrons are excited
questions.correctAnswer: Water molecules are split into $H^+$ and $O_2$ (photolysis) and electrons are excited

questions.question: What is the splitting of water molecules called?
What happens to the oxygen produced during photolysis?
options: It is used in the dark reaction
It is released to the atmosphere through leaf stomata
It is stored in the chloroplast
questions.correctAnswer: It is released to the atmosphere through leaf stomata

questions.question: What molecule is generated by excited electrons passing down the electron transport system during light reaction?
What serves as input in the stroma for converting carbon dioxide to carbohydrate?
options: ATP and $H^+$ harvested during light reaction
questions.correctAnswer: ATP and $H^+$ harvested during light reaction

questions.question: What is the dark reaction also known as?
Can the dark reaction occur in the absence of light?
options: Yes, as long as there is sufficient $H^+$ and ATP from the light reaction
Yes, it needs darkness to function
No, it needs both light and dark
questions.correctAnswer: Yes, as long as there is sufficient $H^+$ and ATP from the light reaction

questions.question: What is the process where $H^+$ indirectly combines with $CO_2$ in the stroma called?
What is the immediate result of the dark reaction?
What is the overall chemical equation of photosynthesis?
What are needed for photosynthesis according to the overall equation?
options: Carbon dioxide, water, chlorophyll and sunlight
questions.correctAnswer: Carbon dioxide, water, chlorophyll and sunlight

questions.question: What are the products of photosynthesis?
What is a simple way of demonstrating food synthesis in leaves?
options: Testing a leaf for starch using iodine solution
questions.correctAnswer: Testing a leaf for starch using iodine solution

questions.question: What colour develops when iodine is applied to starch?
Why is the leaf boiled in water before testing for starch?
options: To kill the cells and stop metabolic activity, and to break down the cuticle
questions.correctAnswer: To kill the cells and stop metabolic activity, and to break down the cuticle

questions.question: Why is the leaf treated with alcohol when testing for starch?
options: To remove the green pigment chlorophyll that interferes with the iodine effect
questions.correctAnswer: To remove the green pigment chlorophyll that interferes with the iodine effect

questions.question: Why is the leaf placed in cold water after alcohol treatment when testing for starch?
options: To make it firm and ready for iodine application
questions.correctAnswer: To make it firm and ready for iodine application
